sprint.name: Turn DAG Phase 4: Active vs Archived Context Split

contracts.title: Context lifecycle and inspection APIs
contracts.note: Add get-by-id, list-by-session/status, and explicit archive/fork inspection helpers so operators can inspect archived contexts without touching live compose APIs.

contracts.title: Explicit archived mining read surfaces
contracts.note: Add explicit archived retrieval/search helpers and avoid widening normal live-path reads behind includeArchived defaults.

contracts.title: Active-only enforcement on compositor and recall paths
contracts.note: Verify standard composition, warming, keystones, FTS, semantic recall, and cross-session context remain active-only.

contracts.title: Routine indexing vs explicit archived mining split
contracts.note: Default background indexing must stay active-only; archived mining requires a separate explicit entrypoint exposed from the HyperMem facade.

contracts.title: Phase 4 regression suite
contracts.note: Prove archived contexts are invisible to standard composition and routine indexing but reachable through explicit archived-mining APIs.

phases_complete: Phase B2 (stable-prefix cache signal propagation and verification)
phase_summary.note: Phase 4 is a product-surface split, not a fresh schema push. The existing context lifecycle fields and context-scoped retrieval primitives should be reused.

next_phase.title: Separate live composition from archived mining
scope: Keep standard composition on the active branch only.
Expose archived context inspection and mining through explicit APIs.
Prevent routine fact extraction and recall from scanning archived branches by accident.
Add regression coverage for active-only live paths and explicit archived-mining paths.
